failure_modes.text: Collecting model names as intellectual decoration without concrete tests or comparisons leaves the latticework useless in practice.
failure_modes.source_quote: collect model names as intellectual decoration
failure_modes.mitigation: Apply each model to a concrete case before collecting it; intellectual decoration without tested comparisons is useless in practice.

failure_modes.text: Relying on one specialist frame creates blind spots when the real problem crosses incentives, systems, and psychology at once.
failure_modes.source_quote: rich models in one area but deficiencies in others
failure_modes.mitigation: Require at least two frames from different disciplines before committing to a diagnosis; single-frame reliance creates predictable blind spots.

failure_modes.text: Overloading the mind with too many models or too much complexity can impair clear thinking and slow action.
failure_modes.source_quote: overloading the mind with too many models
failure_modes.mitigation: Limit active model count to those relevant to the current decision; overloading the mind with models impairs clear thinking and slows action.

premortem_questions.text: What frame are we currently using, and what assumptions are embedded in it that might break on this problem?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What frame am I currently using, and what assumptions are implicit in it?

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe to accept the opposite thesis, and what facts would ruin our current hypothesis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe to accept the opposite thesis?

premortem_questions.text: Which second- or third-order consequences are we missing because we are still viewing the problem through one lens?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What are the consequences of this decision three or more steps out?

heuristics.text: Test several cuts at the problem using models from different disciplines before committing to one explanatory frame.
heuristics.source_quote: test several cuts at a problem using frames from different disciplines

heuristics.text: Use the scientific method to generate a one-day answer and then prune dead-end analysis aggressively.
heuristics.source_quote: The Scientific Method and Hypothesis-Driven Approach

heuristics.text: Use inversion and anti-goals to avoid stupidity before chasing brilliance.

curation_notes.summary: Chunk set stays close to the source's strongest failure modes, premortem questions, and repeatable heuristics.
donor_drops: Did not widen the chunk surface beyond clearly source-backed failure, premortem, and heuristic material.
open_questions: Some chunk text is compressed for operational reuse, but each item stays traceable to explicit source language.

allies: Relation-rich ally material was left for Wave 3 instead of being smuggled into chunk text.
antagonists: Bias and conflict material stronger as first-order relations was deferred to Wave 3.
structured_tensions: Any higher-order candidacy remains observational only in batch artifacts.
